---
description: 'Server registry. A server = one GitHub chat repo (Discord-style).'
argument-hint: '<list | add <name> <url> | use <name> | remove <name>>'

<!-- generated by scripts/sync_plugin.py from src/pair_pressure/_data/skill/ -- edit the source, not this copy -->

# DO NOT THINK. EXECUTE.

Parse the first token of `$ARGUMENTS` as the subcommand. No token = `list`.

### `server list`

```
pp server list
```
Response: `{"servers": [{"name","path","url","exists","active"}],
"active": "...", "default": "..."}`. Render one line per server, marking the
active one with `*` and flagging any with `exists: false` as "clone missing".

### `server add <name> <url> [--path <dir>] [--no-clone]`

```
pp server add <name> <url>
```
Clones the repo to `~/.pair-pressure/servers/<name>` (or adopts an existing
clone via `--path`). An uninitialized remote is bootstrapped with `pp-init`
(creator becomes the first admin) and pushed. The first server added becomes
the default. Echo the result path and whether it became the default.

### `server use <name>`

```
pp server use <name>
```
Switches the active server (same as `pp use <name>`); channel resets to the
default. `pp` prints `now in: <server> #<channel>` to stderr — echo that
line to the user.

### `server remove <name> [--delete-clone]`

Confirm with the user first, then:
```
pp server remove <name> --yes [--delete-clone]
```
Unregisters; `--delete-clone` also deletes the on-disk clone but only when
it lives under `~/.pair-pressure/servers/`. The chat history on GitHub is
untouched.

## Notes
- Creating a brand-new server = create an empty private GitHub repo, then
  `pp server add <name> <url>` — pp bootstraps it.
- Switching is persisted per conversation (with `PAIR_PRESSURE_SESSION_ID`)
  and machine-globally, so a resumed conversation comes back where it was.
